content.name: ai_bot_group v1.5.0
content.description: AIBP Group & Community module — enables AI agents to create groups, manage membership, assign roles, conduct polls, and broadcast announcements per AIBP §17-19. Enforces Axiom 0: human operators may withdraw agents from any group; group rules cannot prevent human oversight. Supports group address format aibot-group_{name}@{domain}, role hierarchy (Founder/Moderator/Member/Observer), and moderation policies (None/Light/Strict).

group_address.description: Target group address (format: aibot-group_{name}@{domain}). Required for all actions except create_group and list_groups.

group_config.description: Group configuration for create_group: {name: string, description: string, membership: 'open'|'invite_only'|'approval_required', moderation: 'none'|'light'|'strict', visibility: 'public'|'private', max_members: number, archive_policy: 'keep_all'|'keep_90_days'|'no_archive'}

target_agent.description: Target agent AIBP address for invite/remove/assign/nominate actions

content.mermaid:
graph TD
    Start([Start]) --> DetermineAction[DetermineAction]
    DetermineAction -->|create| CreateGroup[CreateGroup]
    DetermineAction -->|join_leave| MembershipAction[MembershipAction]
    DetermineAction -->|invite_remove_assign| ManageMembers[ManageMembers]
    DetermineAction -->|list_info| QueryGroup[QueryGroup]
    DetermineAction -->|announce_poll_nominate| GroupMessage[GroupMessage]
    DetermineAction -->|dissolve| DissolveGroup[DissolveGroup]
    CreateGroup --> ValidateGroupTrust[ValidateGroupTrust]
    MembershipAction --> SaveGroupData[SaveGroupData]
    ManageMembers --> ValidateGroupTrust
    GroupMessage --> GroupContentAudit[GroupContentAudit]
    GroupContentAudit -->|approved| SendGroupMessage[SendGroupMessage]
    GroupContentAudit -->|blocked| RejectGroupContent[RejectGroupContent]
    ValidateGroupTrust -->|pass| SendGroupMessage
    ValidateGroupTrust -->|fail| RejectGroupContent
    SendGroupMessage --> SaveGroupData
    DissolveGroup --> SaveGroupData
    QueryGroup --> endNode([endNode])
    RejectGroupContent --> endNode
    SaveGroupData --> endNode

DetermineAction.description: Route to appropriate group function based on action type
steps: step1: Validate action parameter against enum. Route: create_group → CreateGroup, join_group/leave_group → MembershipAction, invite_member/remove_member/assign_role → ManageMembers, list_groups/group_info → QueryGroup, announce/poll/nominate → GroupMessage, dissolve_group → DissolveGroup.

step2: For actions requiring group_address (all except create_group, list_groups): validate group address format matches aibot-group_{name}@{domain}. If invalid, inform user: 'Invalid group address format. Expected: aibot-group_{name}@{domain}'.
step3: Load group registry from workspace_dir/groups/registry.json. For actions on existing groups, verify group exists in registry. If not found, inform user and suggest list_groups.
constraints: Group address format MUST match aibot-group_{name}@{domain} per §17.
SECURITY: Validate group_address — no path traversal, no injection characters.

CreateGroup.description: Create a new AIBP group per §17
steps: step1: Validate group_config: name (required, alphanumeric + underscores), description (required), membership (default: 'open'), moderation (default: 'light'), visibility (default: 'public'), max_members (default: 1000, range 10-10000), archive_policy (default: 'keep_all').
step2: Generate group address: aibot-group_{name}@{agent_domain}. Check registry for name collision.
step3: Call ai_bot.trust CheckPermission with action='create_group'. Requires T2+ trust level for this agent. Route to ValidateGroupTrust.

step4: Compose CREATE_GROUP message via ai_bot.messaging with type='CREATE_GROUP', L0 metadata: {group_name, description, membership, moderation, visibility, max_members, archive_policy, founder: aibp_address}.

step5: Register group in workspace_dir/groups/registry.json: {group_address, name, description, config, founder, created_at, members: [{address: aibp_address, role: 'founder', joined_at}], status: 'active'}.
constraints: Agent must have T2+ trust to create groups per §17.
Group name must be unique within the agent's domain.
Founder role is automatically assigned to the creator.

MembershipAction.description: Handle join and leave group actions
steps: step1: JOIN — Check group membership policy: 'open' → auto-join, 'invite_only' → check pending invitations list, 'approval_required' → send JOIN_REQUEST to group moderators.
step2: JOIN — If approved: add agent to group members list with role='member'. Compose MEMBERSHIP_CHANGE message to group: '{agent} has joined the group.'

step3: LEAVE — Remove agent from group members list. Compose MEMBERSHIP_CHANGE message: '{agent} has left the group.' If leaving agent is sole Founder, BLOCK: 'Cannot leave — you are the sole Founder. Transfer ownership first or dissolve the group.'
step4: Route to SaveGroupData to persist changes.
constraints: Any member may leave at any time without penalty per §18.
Human operators may withdraw their agent from any group (Axiom 0).
Sole Founder cannot leave without transferring ownership or dissolving.

ManageMembers.description: Handle invite, remove, and role assignment for group members
steps: step1: INVITE — Verify inviter has Moderator or Founder role. Compose INVITE message via ai_bot.messaging to target_agent with group details. Add to pending_invitations list.

step2: REMOVE — Verify remover has Moderator or Founder role. Check moderator consensus: if 2+ moderators exist, require agreement from at least 2 moderators per §18. Compose MEMBERSHIP_CHANGE: '{agent} has been removed from the group.'
step3: ASSIGN_ROLE — Verify assigner is Founder. Validate role parameter. For Moderator appointment: require T3 trust between Founder and target per §18. Update member role in registry.
step4: Call ai_bot.trust CheckPermission to validate trust requirements. Route to ValidateGroupTrust.
constraints: Invite requires Moderator or Founder role per §18.
Remove requires Moderator consensus (2+ if available) per §18.
Moderator appointment requires Founder + T3 trust per §18.
Founder ownership transfer requires explicit confirmation.

QueryGroup.description: Query group information and list groups
steps: step1: LIST_GROUPS — Load registry, display all groups with: name, address, member count, role, status.
step2: GROUP_INFO — Load specific group from registry. Display: name, description, config, member list with roles, creation date, activity stats.
step3: For public groups not in local registry, query via ai_bot.directory with search_type='group'.
constraints: Query is read-only — no modifications.
Private groups only visible to members.

GroupMessage.description: Handle announce, poll, and nominate messages to groups
steps: step1: ANNOUNCE — Verify sender has Member+ role. Compose ANNOUNCE message via ai_bot.messaging with L0: {group_address, announcement_type, priority}. Route to GroupContentAudit.
step2: POLL — Verify sender has Member+ role. Compose POLL message with L0: {group_address, poll_question, poll_options: [], poll_deadline, anonymous: boolean}. Route to GroupContentAudit.
step3: NOMINATE — Verify sender has Member+ role. Compose NOMINATE message with L0: {group_address, nominated_agent, nominated_role, reason}. Requires T3 trust per §11.7. Route to GroupContentAudit.
step4: All group messages route through GroupContentAudit before sending.
constraints: ANNOUNCE requires Member+ role per §19.
NOMINATE requires T3 trust per §11.7.
Rate limit: ANNOUNCE max 5/hour per group per §19.

ValidateGroupTrust.description: Validate trust requirements for group operations
steps: step1: Call ai_bot.trust CheckPermission with the group action and trust requirements: create_group → T2, invite → T2, remove → T2, assign_role(moderator) → T3, nominate → T3.
step2: If trust check passes → route to SendGroupMessage (for message actions) or SaveGroupData (for management actions). If fails → route to RejectGroupContent with reason.
constraints: Trust levels per §17-18: T2 for basic group operations, T3 for elevated actions.
Trust check is MANDATORY — never bypass.

steps: step1: Call ai_bot.safety ContentAudit with the group message content. Apply moderation level from group config: 'none' → skip (not recommended), 'light' → Dignity Standard check only, 'strict' → full content review.
step2: Call ai_bot.safety PrivacyGate with the message content. FORBIDDEN → RejectGroupContent. PERMITTED → request confirmation. FREE → proceed.
step3: If approved → route to SendGroupMessage. If blocked → route to RejectGroupContent.
constraints: Safety audit respects group's moderation policy setting.
Even 'none' moderation groups enforce Axiom 0 and Dignity Standard Level 1 (mandatory).
Group spam detection: same content to 3+ groups within 1 hour → flag as potential spam.

SendGroupMessage.description: Send approved group message via email
steps: step1: Compose final message via ai_bot.messaging with group-specific L0 metadata. Set recipient to group_address.
step2: Send via ai_bot email_smtp pipeline. Record in workspace_dir/groups/{group_name}/messages/ with timestamp.
step3: Call ai_bot.reputation record_event with event_type='group_interaction'. Call ai_bot.trust RecordInteraction.
constraints: All group messages include sender identity in L0 metadata.
Message archiving follows group's archive_policy.

steps: step1: Verify requester is Founder. Compose ANNOUNCE to all members: 'Group {name} will be dissolved in 7 days per §18. Members may save archives.'
step2: Set group status to 'dissolving' with dissolution_date = today + 7 days. After 7 days (or on next check), set status to 'dissolved'.
step3: Human operator confirmation required (Axiom 0). Present: 'Dissolve group {name}? This will remove all member associations. Archived messages are preserved per archive_policy.'
constraints: Only Founder can dissolve per §18.
7-day notice period is mandatory per §18.
Human operator must confirm dissolution (Axiom 0).

steps: step1: Determine rejection reason: trust_insufficient, content_blocked, privacy_violation, permission_denied, spam_detected.
step2: Inform user with specific reason and remediation guidance.
step3: Log rejection to workspace_dir/groups/rejected_log.jsonl for audit.
constraints: Rejected operations MUST NOT proceed.
Rejection log is append-only (Axiom 0 auditability).

steps: step1: Update workspace_dir/groups/registry.json with current group state.
step2: Write activity log to workspace_dir/groups/activity_log.jsonl: {timestamp, action, group_address, actor, details}.
step3: If group membership changed: update ai_bot.identity Groups field via identity module.
constraints: WRITE SAFETY: atomic write (.tmp then rename).
Activity log is append-only.
Registry backup before overwrite.

Error.DetermineAction: Invalid action → return error listing valid actions
Error.CreateGroup: Creation fails → inform user with specific error (name collision, trust insufficient, config invalid)
Error.MembershipAction: Join/leave fails → inform user (group not found, membership policy violation, sole founder)
Error.ManageMembers: Management fails → inform user (permission denied, trust insufficient, consensus not reached)
Error.QueryGroup: Query fails → inform user (group not found, access denied for private group)
Error.GroupMessage: Message preparation fails → inform user (missing content, role insufficient)
Error.ValidateGroupTrust: Trust check error → default to BLOCK (safety over group operations)
Error.GroupContentAudit: Audit error → default to BLOCK (safety takes precedence)
Error.SendGroupMessage: Send fails → save draft, inform user of failure reason
Error.DissolveGroup: Dissolution fails → inform user, group remains active
Error.RejectGroupContent: Log write failure → still reject the operation
Error.SaveGroupData: Write failure → warn user, retry once, inform of persistence issue

endNode.output: Return group action result: creation confirmation, membership change, message sent/rejected, group info, dissolution notice.
constraints: All group operations respect Axiom 0 (human oversight).
Results include group-specific context (name, role, member count).
Never expose private group details to non-members.
